h3ad51e82g2de1g44dega484g3fcb989af011: Do you feel as flattered as I do? A cambion came courting us.
haf3c6aa8g2acag411fg96bfg95c55bbea27b: You have a strange definition of courtship.
h643197adg89aag4b0aga4cdgdb80d77edfc0: Believe me, that was a devil's equivalent of serenades and roses.
h7c3523a6g7678g4ebag8974g230fd0309158: Don't let his bluster fool you. All that talk of desperation? It merely illustrates his own.
h75173284gd475g4182g9558g184761192332: I think he wants something from us. Badly. And in that knowledge lies our opportunity.
hdf03a2b7g03beg43b5ga636g42e46f0d6936: Conjecture, yes - that much I concede.
ha8d40f59g5f01g44e4g9e60gb83fc98d04d9: But let me play the devil deal's advocate: the man is too eager. Do not dismiss his offer out of hand.
he1fe1031gd8c4g49d3gbe6ag76f49a144015: Raphael is a cambion, which makes him part human. And what is human, is fallible.
h4da06fd4g1903g477ag845cg4cb2b170f60e: Fine - but my reasoning is this:
hf9c27900g2d57g40f4gb902g19efea8d4faa: Fact one: there's something very strange and very powerful about our tadpoles.
h3c248206g62dbg40ffg89efgee27a8ec74b7: Fact two: a devil offers to take it away.
h04634c11g35b9g48f0g92afgb72b311db1f8: What if the tadpole is what he really wants instead of the customary price that is our souls?
hb349630dg6882g4522gb5d0gd643bc51a472: If I'm right, there's a mighty bargain to be made.
h6bea3144g54e7g45a7gbcb1g98ed6c0deffe: Remember his Cormyrian rhyme? 'Down came the claw'. Perhaps we should start growing our nails.
h1240ae75g0641g4700gaa7cgb9519de5c265: I don't quite catch your drift.
h4843e0d3gf817g4f75gac1cg3279aae6aef9: And how do you propose we beat a devil at his own game?
hb919f257g84c9g4eb5gbdbfg7ae23c71b847: By figuring out his true intentions.
h576e21d3g1708g4a1bg8b47ga0a9415bcb55: Raphael is a cambion. And what is part human, is entirely fallible.
hfbe6bd65g7a55g4397g8d36g3307b7cbf6b2: But what is it that this devil wants so very badly?
h8053c76bg5b63g4f2fga84dg8ea7d76c156b: Our souls. But I suspect that's but his opening offer.
h2d73eacfg2211g4beeg8183g3d8900acf4ba: Let me play the devil deal's advocate: the man is too eager. Do not dismiss his offer out of hand.
hd4bf83b3g05fdg403cgb1bagf81b2287c26c: I'll remind you that when dealing with a devil, you always draw the short straw.
h23b51871g3ea2g4c3eg8614gb0cbfc887864: There's no such thing as an absolute certainty.
h61651848g91d3g41c1gbddbg57e2a641ad0a: He only wants what all devils want: our souls.
hd9e89b81g6d46g4296gabc0g9ef22777d732: Dining with a devil left a bad taste in my mouth.
hac97dfbcg32bcg4a9cg9092g1d40214709aa: Yes... They offer you an apple, but they're the worm that lurks within.
h06b62acdgd042g4f93g9c90g171b859115ff: Still - don't let his bluster fool you. All that talk of desperation? It merely illustrates his own.
h12a29586g98a1g478bg977fg06142e2691a9: What's a cambion in the first place?
h773d07f0g7a5ag40a0g9f55g5da958bc2828: Cambions are half-human. Either daddy had a tryst with a succubus or mommy with an incubus.
hada941e2geb58g4cf1g9d2cg2f1e03210d14: What they lack in kind-heartedness, they make up for with horns and tails.
